name: Otter Elite Force — Full Engine Rewrite (SolidJS + LittleJS + bitECS)
description: Migrate from React+Konva+Koota to SolidJS+LittleJS+bitECS per engine-rewrite-plan.md v3.0. Foundation complete; this covers Milestones B through G.

userStories.title: Skirmish runtime sandbox
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a skirmish mode that boots a minimal battlefield with deterministic seed so I can test systems in isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Skirmish config specifies seed, map size, faction placement
Skirmish session boots GameWorld with entities from config
Systems pipeline runs in skirmish mode
Diagnostics snapshot emitted on skirmish completion
Seed replay produces identical entity state
pnpm test passes
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Vitest browser visual capture harness
userStories.description: As a developer, I want deterministic visual snapshot tests so I can catch rendering regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Vitest browser test boots a skirmish session in headless browser
Captures canvas screenshot at tick 0, tick 60, tick 300
Compares against baseline images with pixel diff threshold
Fails on regression above threshold
pnpm test:browser passes

userStories.title: Port core gameplay systems to GameWorld
userStories.description: As a developer, I want movement, combat, economy, production, fog, AI, and order systems reading/writing GameWorld so the game loop simulates.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/systems/ contains: movementSystem.ts, combatSystem.ts, economySystem.ts, productionSystem.ts, fogSystem.ts, aiSystem.ts, orderSystem.ts
Each system is a pure function taking GameWorld
Movement reads order queues, moves entity toward target at speed per tick
Combat finds nearest enemy in attack range, applies damage, marks dead for removal
Economy: workers near resources increment world.session.resources
Production: buildings advance queue progress, spawn unit at 100%
Fog: maintains Uint8Array grid, marks tiles around player faction entities visible
AI: enemy entities with no orders seek nearest player entity in detection range
flushRemovals called at end of system pipeline
Add Attack.range, Attack.cooldown, Attack.lastAttackTick to components.ts if missing
Unit tests for each system in adjacent .test.ts files
pnpm test passes
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Mission bootstrap pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want bootstrapMission(world, missionId) that loads a mission definition and seeds GameWorld with all placements, resources, objectives, terrain dimensions, and script tags.
acceptanceCriteria: bootstrapMission reads mission from registry by ID
Spawns all placements via spawnUnit/spawnBuilding/spawnResource
Sets world.session.resources to mission starting values
Sets world.navigation.width/height from mission map size
Registers scriptId tags for named entities
Sets initial objectives on world.session.objectives
Sets world.session.phase to playing
Test: bootstrap Mission 1, verify entity count, positions, resources, objectives
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: System pipeline wired into runtime tick loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the tick() function to run all gameplay systems each frame so entities move, fight, gather, and die.
acceptanceCriteria: RuntimeHost creates system pipeline alongside mission flow
onTick callback runs: mission flow step then system pipeline step
Entities move toward targets each frame visibly
Combat resolves — dead entities removed from canvas
Resources accumulate in HUD from worker gathering
Bridge state reflects live game state each tick
pnpm test passes
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Terrain tile rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the tactical canvas to render Kenney tiles instead of grid lines so terrain is visible.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/rendering/terrainRenderer.ts created
Tile images loaded from public/assets/tiles/ using import.meta.env.BASE_URL
Terrain grid rendered at 32x32 per cell with camera offset and zoom
Viewport culling — only visible tiles drawn
Basic terrain types: grass, water, sand, forest, dirt, stone
Chunks pre-rendered to offscreen canvases for performance
Integrated into drawScene() in littlejsRuntime.ts
Falls back to grid if tiles not loaded yet
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Sprite atlas entity rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want entities rendered with sprite atlas frames instead of colored shapes.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/rendering/spriteRenderer.ts created
Sprite atlas JSON+PNG loaded per animal type from public/assets/sprites/
Entity type maps to correct atlas using existing animal mapping
Sprites rendered at correct position, sorted by Y for depth
Animation frames cycle based on tick count
Selection circles and HP bars rendered on top of sprites
Falls back to colored shapes if atlas not loaded
Integrated into drawScene() in littlejsRuntime.ts
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Fog of war rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want fog of war drawn as an overlay on the tactical canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/rendering/fogRenderer.ts created
Unexplored tiles render as black
Explored tiles render at 50% opacity dark overlay
Visible tiles render transparent
Fog aligns to 32x32 grid with camera offset
Entities in unexplored fog are not rendered
Minimap reflects fog state
Integrated into drawScene() after entity rendering
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Save/load roundtrip on new runtime
userStories.description: As a developer, I want mid-mission save and load working via SQLite so progress is not lost.
acceptanceCriteria: Save serializes all entity scalar state from bitECS typed arrays
Save serializes world session state, runtime queues, campaign progress
Load reconstructs GameWorld from saved data
Runtime-only objects (Yuka vehicles, nav graph references) reconstructed after load
Mission continues from saved tick with correct entity positions
Test: save at tick 100, load into new GameWorld, verify entity positions match
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Input consolidation — desktop and mobile
userStories.description: As a developer, I want one coherent input path for desktop and mobile with all RTS interactions working.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop: left-click select, drag-box select, right-click context command, right-drag pan, scroll zoom
Mobile: tap select/command, long-press drag-select, two-finger pan, pinch zoom
Minimap click/drag jumps camera on both platforms
Context-sensitive commands: attack enemy, gather resource, enter building
No edge scroll anywhere
Control groups: Ctrl+1..9 assign, 1..9 recall
Keyboard hotkeys as accelerators only (on-screen control must exist for every action)
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Port secondary systems to GameWorld
userStories.description: As a developer, I want stealth, detection, water, boss, convoy, wave spawner, and weather systems ported.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/systems/ contains: stealthSystem, detectionSystem, waterSystem, bossSystem, convoySystem, waveSpawnerSystem, weatherSystem
Each is a pure function on GameWorld
Unit tests for each system pass
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Port mission-specific systems to GameWorld
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tidal, fire, siphon, multiBase, territory, demolition, siege, loot, encounter, difficultyScaling, and scoring systems ported.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/systems/ contains all 11 mission-specific systems
Each operates on GameWorld as pure function
Unit tests pass for each
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Chapter 1 missions (1-4) fully playable
userStories.description: As a player, I want Missions 1-4 to boot, play through, and complete on the new runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Mission 1 Beachhead: boot, triggers, objectives, victory/defeat
Mission 2 Causeway: bridge mechanics, zone progression
Mission 3 Firebase Delta: defensive position, wave defense
Mission 4 Prison Break: rescue objectives, escort mechanics
Each tested: bootstrap → play → complete
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Chapter 2 missions (5-8) fully playable
userStories.description: As a player, I want Missions 5-8 playable with chapter-specific mechanics.
acceptanceCriteria: Mission 5 Siphon Valley: siphon mechanic operational
Mission 6 Monsoon Ambush: weather system active
Mission 7 River Rats: water/tidal mechanics
Mission 8 Underwater Cache: submersion mechanics
Each tested end-to-end
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Chapter 3 missions (9-12) fully playable
userStories.description: As a player, I want Missions 9-12 playable with fire, entrenchment, rescue, siphon mechanics.
acceptanceCriteria: Mission 9 Dense Canopy: stealth/canopy mechanics
Mission 10 Scorched Earth: fire spread system
Mission 11 Entrenchment: siege mechanics
Mission 12 Fang Rescue: escort/rescue objectives
Each tested end-to-end
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Chapter 4 missions (13-16) fully playable
userStories.description: As a player, I want Missions 13-16 playable including the final boss encounter.
acceptanceCriteria: Mission 13 Great Siphon: multi-base system
Mission 14 Iron Delta: fortress assault
Mission 15 Serpent Lair: boss system with phases
Mission 16 Last Stand: escalation and final victory
Each tested end-to-end
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Campaign flow end-to-end
userStories.description: As a player, I want to start a new campaign, play missions in sequence, see briefings, track progress, and reach the ending.
acceptanceCriteria: Main menu → New Campaign → Mission 1 briefing → play → victory → Mission 2 → ...
Campaign progress persists to SQLite between sessions
Mission unlock progression works correctly
Star ratings recorded per mission
Campaign completion screen after Mission 16 victory
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Skirmish mode fully functional
userStories.description: As a player, I want skirmish mode with configurable seed, map, factions, and difficulty.
acceptanceCriteria: Skirmish setup screen: seed input, shuffle button, faction select, map size, difficulty
Skirmish session boots and plays with all systems
Skirmish result screen shows stats (units lost, resources gathered, duration)
Skirmish setup persists to SQLite
Seed replay produces deterministic identical results
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: SolidJS app root and routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the app root to be SolidJS with screen routing replacing React.
acceptanceCriteria: src/main.tsx renders Solid root via render() from solid-js/web
Screen routing implemented in Solid (main-menu, campaign, settings, skirmish, game, briefing, result)
No React imports in the app shell routing path
Vite config updated for solid-js JSX
pnpm build passes
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: SolidJS main menu and campaign view
userStories.description: As a player, I want main menu and campaign selection as SolidJS components.
acceptanceCriteria: MainMenu component in Solid with New Campaign, Continue, Skirmish, Settings buttons
CampaignView component in Solid showing mission list with unlock/star state
Mission selection triggers briefing flow
Components styled with existing Tailwind classes
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: SolidJS settings and skirmish setup
userStories.description: As a player, I want settings and skirmish setup as Solid components.
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsPanel in Solid: audio volume sliders, subtitles toggle, reduce motion toggle
SkirmishSetup in Solid: seed input, shuffle, faction, map size, difficulty, launch
Settings persist to SQLite via persistence store
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: SolidJS briefing and result overlays
userStories.description: As a player, I want mission briefing, pause, and result overlays as Solid components.
acceptanceCriteria: BriefingOverlay in Solid: commander portrait, mission description, objectives preview, deploy button
PauseOverlay in Solid: resume, settings, quit buttons
MissionResult in Solid: victory/defeat banner, star rating, next mission button
SkirmishResult in Solid: stats summary, replay seed display, rematch button
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: SolidJS tactical HUD
userStories.description: As a player, I want the in-game HUD as Solid components reading from GameBridge signals.
acceptanceCriteria: ResourceBar in Solid: fish, timber, salvage counts, population current/max
SelectionPanel in Solid: selected entity info, portrait, stats, action buttons
BuildMenu in Solid: building options grid when worker selected
AlertBanner in Solid: tactical alert messages with severity colors
ObjectivesPanel in Solid: mission objectives with status indicators
BossHealthBar in Solid: boss name and HP bar
CommandTransmissionPanel in Solid: dialogue speaker and text
ErrorFeedback in Solid: error messages
All read from GameBridge Solid signals, not polling
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: GameBridge with Solid signals
userStories.description: As a developer, I want GameBridge to use Solid createSignal/createStore for fine-grained reactive HUD updates.
acceptanceCriteria: GameBridge exposes Solid Accessor-based state (resources, population, selection, objectives, alerts, dialogue, weather, boss)
Bridge updated from game loop via batch() at end of each tick
No polling interval — HUD reacts to signal changes
Emit methods (pause, resume, startBuild, queueUnit, etc.) dispatch to game runtime
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: SolidJS mobile-adaptive components
userStories.description: As a mobile player, I want touch-optimized command buttons, radial menu, and squad tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: CommandButtons in Solid: large tap targets for common actions
RadialMenu in Solid: context menu on long-press for unit commands
SquadTabs in Solid: quick unit group switching
Layout adapts by form factor: phone prioritizes large targets, tablet shows more info, desktop shows full panels
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Audio migration from React hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Tone.js audio wired without React hooks.
acceptanceCriteria: useAudioUnlock, useMusicWiring, useAudioSettings React hooks removed
Audio unlock triggers from Solid lifecycle or first user interaction event
Music and SFX controlled via world events or bridge emit calls
Volume settings read from bridge/world.settings
pnpm build passes
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Polish pass — all 16 missions
userStories.description: As a player, I want all 16 missions polished for pacing, readability, audio, fog/minimap, and balance.
acceptanceCriteria: Each mission reviewed against its design doc in docs/missions/
Dialogue triggers fire at documented moments
Audio cues play for combat, alerts, objectives, phase changes
Fog and minimap are readable and accurate
Balance tuned via diagnostics-driven analysis
No visual artifacts, stuck units, or broken triggers in any mission

userStories.title: Delete legacy React runtime
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all React components, hooks, and React-specific code deleted from the codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ui/ directory deleted (all React HUD, briefing, command-post, mobile, layout components)
React app root in src/app/App.tsx deleted or replaced with Solid
React-specific hooks deleted
react, react-dom removed from package.json dependencies
@testing-library/react removed from devDependencies
Grep confirms zero imports of react or react-dom in src/
pnpm build passes
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Delete legacy Koota ECS runtime
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all Koota ECS code deleted.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ecs/world.ts, src/ecs/traits/, src/ecs/relations/, src/ecs/queries/ deleted
src/ecs/singletons.ts deleted
src/systems/ (old Koota systems) deleted — replaced by src/engine/systems/
koota, @koota/react removed from package.json
Grep confirms zero imports of koota or @koota/react in src/
pnpm build passes
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Delete legacy Konva references
userStories.description: As a developer, I want any remaining react-konva/konva references purged.
acceptanceCriteria: react-konva removed from package.json
konva removed from package.json if present
Grep confirms zero imports of react-konva or konva in src/
pnpm build passes

userStories.title: Test migration — Koota/React to bitECS/Solid
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all tests ported from Koota/React test fixtures to bitECS/Solid fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: All src/__tests__/systems/ tests rewritten to create GameWorld and use bitECS components
All src/__tests__/ecs/ tests rewritten against bitECS component stores
All src/__tests__/specs/ui/ tests rewritten for Solid components
@testing-library/react removed or replaced with solid-testing-library
All 2449+ tests pass: pnpm test
No test file imports koota, @koota/react, react, or react-dom

userStories.title: Visual regression and device tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want visual regression tests and Maestro device tests in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: Vitest browser: canvas snapshots for Mission 1 at tick 0, 60, 300 compared to baselines
Playwright: campaign flow E2E test (menu → briefing → play → victory)
Playwright: responsive viewport checks (phone, tablet, desktop)
Maestro: Android skirmish launch flow (tap start → select skirmish → play)
Maestro: tap-select-move flow on Android
CI workflow runs all three test layers
pnpm test:browser passes

userStories.title: Diagnostics fully operational
userStories.description: As a developer, I want diagnostics JSON emitted per mission/skirmish run for macro/meso/micro analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Run metadata captured: mission ID, seed phrase, design seed, gameplay seeds, duration
Objective transition events logged with tick
Encounter and wave roll outcomes logged
Performance counters populated: FPS, frame time, system time
Pathfinding anomalies tracked: stuck entities, boundary violations
Fog and minimap stat counters collected
All data persisted to SQLite diagnostic_snapshot table
pnpm test passes

userStories.title: Documentation updated to final state
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all docs reflecting only the new SolidJS/LittleJS/bitECS stack.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md references only SolidJS, LittleJS, bitECS
AGENTS.md architecture table shows only new stack
docs/architecture/ docs updated for final runtime
No references to React, Konva, or Koota in active docs
CONTRIBUTING.md updated for new dev workflow and file structure
docs/engine-rewrite-plan.md status changed to complete

userStories.title: Clean dependency graph — zero legacy in prod
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the production bundle free of legacy frameworks.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm build succeeds with zero warnings about legacy imports
Bundle audit: no react, react-dom, react-konva, konva, koota, @koota/react in any prod chunk
package.json: all legacy deps removed from dependencies (may keep in devDeps temporarily for test tooling migration)
pnpm test passes with clean dependency graph
pnpm lint passes
